notes: Classic very low-mass arm. The fixed headshell version of the Improved S2. Ideal for high-compliance cartridges like Shure V15 III.  The classic low-mass arm with a removable headshell, making it slightly heavier than the fixed version. Still best for high-compliance cartridges.  A benchmark for budget to mid-priced tonearms for decades. VTA requires shims. Rega's geometry is a Stevenson variant.  Legendary heavy-mass arm from Technics with variable dynamic damping. Excellent for low-compliance MCs like the Denon DL-103.  A very popular and versatile modern medium-to-heavy S-shaped arm. Features oil damping.  The classic tonearm for the Linn Sondek LP12. Known for its rhythmic and dynamic performance. Medium mass.  A legendary heavy-mass arm, considered one of the best ever for low-compliance MCs. Built like a tank.  A popular low-mass arm from Pro-Ject, featuring a one-piece carbon fiber armtube and headshell.  Iconic 'quad-master' ultra-low-mass arm from the 70s. Instantly recognizable design.  The legendary ultra-low-mass champion. Made with thin carbon fiber tubes. Fragile but a perfect match for extremely high-compliance cartridges like the ADC XLM/ZLM.  En klassisk, tung S-formad tonarm, lämplig för Ortofon's egna SPU-kassetter. Information från verifierad tredjepartskälla.  A modern reference tonearm. One-piece magnesium arm tube. Known for its neutrality and detail retrieval.  VPI's 9-inch unipivot tonearm. A popular choice on their turntables. Low-to-medium mass.  Represents a typical modern, budget-friendly S-shaped arm. Medium mass.  A highly regarded unipivot arm known for its stability and excellent sound. Medium mass.  A classic British unipivot tonearm. Low mass, often seen as a great partner for Decca cartridges.  A vintage Japanese arm famous for its 'double knife edge' bearing. Medium-heavy mass. The classic stock tonearm on many Thorens turntables like the TD 160. Medium mass. Linn's reference tonearm. Built with titanium and extremely tight tolerances. A modern classic. Represents the typical medium-mass arm found on classic Dual automatic turntables. A classic and highly regarded Japanese tonearm, known for its precision and 'on-the-fly' VTA adjustment. Legendary British unipivot arm. Cherished for its musicality and 'boogie factor'. Famously has no anti-skate. A well-regarded medium-mass tonearm from the 90s, often seen as a competitor to the Rega RB300. En modern kolfibertonarm med ett gimbal-lager, designad för att vara styv och låg-resonans. Medeltung. A high-end German tonearm known for its precision engineering and Cardanic bearing. Medium mass. The modern entry-level Rega arm, found on Planar 1 and Planar 2. Shares geometry with the RB3xx series. A legendary and highly-regarded Scottish tonearm, often considered a peer to the Linn Ittok. Known for its fluid, musical sound. A classic high-end tonearm famous for its one-piece titanium armtube. Known for its strength, stability, and clean sound. Mobile Fidelity's 10-inch tonearm, developed with Allen Perkins of Spiral Groove. Medium mass. A legendary and innovative low-mass tonearm from the late 70s. Famous for its fluid damping and superb sound quality. The original classic 12-inch arm, ideal for low-compliance cartridges like the Ortofon SPU. A modular system with interchangeable armwands (e.g., EPA-A501H for high mass). Base effective mass is for the EPA-A501M wand. A highly-regarded modern unipivot tonearm with exceptional stability and 'on-the-fly' VTA adjustment. A legendary Japanese 12-inch high-mass tonearm, prized for its dynamic and powerful sound with low-compliance MCs. Modern Thorens tonearm with magnetic anti-skating, found on their higher-end turntables. A high-quality vintage arm from Audio-Technica with dynamic damping. A highly-regarded British tonearm, modified from a Rega base. Known for its superb musicality and fluid sound. Classic 'S-shaped' Denon tonearm with dynamic damping, often paired with the DP-series turntables. En välbyggd tonarm med medelhög effektiv massa, lämplig för lågkomplianspatroner.  Designen är inspirerad av klassiska sändartonarmar. A high-end vintage arm from Sony's golden era, featuring a carbon fiber tube. A unique vintage arm where the entire carbon fiber tube could be swapped for different effective masses. Medium mass is for the standard wand. Iconic design with a golf ball suspended in silicone fluid for damping. A 'damped unipivot'. Another classic British ultra-low-mass unipivot arm, a direct competitor to the Hadcock and Infinity Black Widow. The classic 10-inch studio tonearm from EMT, designed for their TSD series cartridges. A unique vintage arm with a pivoting headshell designed to eliminate tracking error. A complex but fascinating design. A classic linear tracking tonearm that uses a quartz rod and rides on a track. Extremely low mass. A modern, high-precision German tonearm with a dual-layer carbon fiber armtube. A legendary and very expensive servo-controlled linear tracking tonearm. A highly regarded Japanese unipivot tonearm with oil damping. Known for its clean and musical sound. A low-mass unipivot from Grace, an alternative to the G-707 with a removable headshell. A legendary, ultra-high-end Swiss tonearm. Extremely rare and revered for its precision and sound quality. The tonearm on the modern, audiophile-oriented Technics SL-1200GR. A solid medium-mass performer. The infamous stock arm on the classic Lenco L75. Very high mass, with vertical V-block bearings that often need replacing. Roksan's unipivot tonearm, known for a lively, engaging, and musical presentation. A low-mass, unipivot tonearm from speaker manufacturer Magnepan, featuring a unique design. A well-built vintage Japanese tonearm, often paired with their own high-output MC cartridges. Same as Mayware Formula IV. Ultra-low-mass unipivot arm. A very high-quality, heavy-mass Japanese tonearm from the 70s. Superb engineering. A legendary and rare high-end arm, also known as 'The Arm' by Sumiko. Designed by David Fletcher. A modern high-end tonearm featuring a unique tri-point bearing. Medium mass. Represents the typical medium-heavy stock arm on vintage Pioneer turntables. A later, 'Low Cost' version of the famous 774 arm. A straight, low-mass arm that was a great performer for its price. Pro-Ject's 12-inch reference unipivot tonearm. Medium-heavy mass. A popular and well-made vintage Japanese tonearm. Solid, reliable medium-mass performer. The original, heavier Series II arm. Medium mass, a great match for a wider range of cartridges than the later 'Improved' version, including the Denon DL-103. A unique bi-axis tonearm with separate arms for horizontal and vertical movement, designed for exceptional tracking. A high-performance modern Rega arm, featuring a tapered armtube and extremely low-tolerance bearings. Found on the Planar 8. A classic, affordable, and well-performing S-shaped tonearm. A popular choice for many turntable projects. Linn's entry-level tonearm for many years. A solid, reliable performer with medium mass. VPI's 12-inch reference unipivot tonearm. Medium mass, excellent stability and sound. A modern, high-precision 9-inch tonearm from Ortofon. Very low mass despite the removable headshell, making it versatile. A high-quality vintage arm from AT's professional line. Medium-heavy mass. A rare and beautiful 12-inch unipivot tonearm from Grace with a wooden armtube. A very high-end vintage arm from Micro Seiki, known for its superb build quality and sonic performance. A long-running and successful tonearm from Roksan, known for its clever design and engaging sound. A modern, reference-level American tonearm, famous for its precision VTA adjustment tower. A simplified version of the reference Series V, offering a large portion of its performance at a lower price point. A 9-inch version of the legendary 4Point tonearm, which uses a unique 4-point bearing system for unipivot-like freedom and gimbal-like stability. A popular unipivot arm often bundled with Nottingham Analogue turntables. Known for its steady, musical presentation. The stock arm on the RPM 9 turntable. A conical carbon fiber tube over an aluminum core for stiffness and damping. An early and influential carbon fiber unipivot design from the British speaker manufacturer. A classic Danish unipivot tonearm with interchangeable armwands for different effective masses. This is for the standard light wand. A high-quality tonearm found on Luxman's high-end turntables from the 70s and 80s. A common tonearm from the Czech manufacturer, found on many vintage turntables from the Eastern Bloc. A vintage 12-inch professional studio tonearm from Sony. A well-regarded, affordable vintage tonearm from Jelco. Medium-heavy mass. A heavily modified Rega arm from Michell Engineering. The armtube is bead-blasted and perforated, and the wiring is upgraded. A classic 12-inch tonearm for professional use, a direct competitor to the SME 3012. A long-running mid-tier Linn tonearm, positioned between the Basik/Krane and the Ekos. VPI's modern high-end gimbal-bearing tonearm, an alternative to their classic unipivots. A light-to-medium mass gimbal-bearing arm from Grace. En tidig, lätt tonarm från det skotska företaget som senare tillverkade PU2 och PU3.  Information hämtad från en verifierad tredjepartskälla. A very popular carbon fiber tonearm from Micro Seiki, known for its clean aesthetics and sound. Legendary tonearm with a unique magnetic thread bearing. Prized for its natural and effortless sound. Captive Bearing model. A more conventional design using high-precision gimbal bearings, known for its dynamic and stable sound. Unik svängarmsdesign med en 'pivot-on-a-pivot'-konstruktion för att uppnå verkligt linjär spårning utan luftlager eller servo. Teoretiskt sett noll spårningsfel.  Information verifierad från en oberoende databas. Tangential-pivot tonearm using a Thales geometry principle with a laser for setup assistance. Aims for zero tracking error. Features a unique combination of gimbal and magnetic stabilization. Allows for on-the-fly VTA and azimuth adjustment. Reed's entry-level model, focusing on a simple, rigid design with high-quality gimbal bearings. Swedish ultra-high-end tonearm, famous for its extremely rigid, large-diameter carbon fiber armtube. Vertere's flagship tonearm featuring a unique tri-point unipivot bearing and composite armtube. A high-quality German tonearm with a carbon fiber over aluminum armtube. Modern 12-inch high-mass tonearm from the Japanese brand formerly known as Abis. Excellent for low-compliance cartridges. A high-end linear tracking tonearm employing an air bearing for the carriage and magnetic suspension for the arm tube.  This design aims for exceptional tracking accuracy and minimal distortion. A modern, ultra-high-end 12-inch tonearm from Japan, known for its massive stainless steel construction. German 12-inch Turbo tonearm with a unique dual-pivot horizontal bearing. Swiss tangential-pivot tonearm with a unique geometry that reduces tracking error to a claimed maximum of 0.006 degrees. A classic American unipivot tonearm, known for its stability and neutral sound profile. En japansktillverkad tonarm, föregångare till Sorane. En högkvalitativ, medeltung statisk balanserad tonarm. German high-end tonearm with a unique Nanotube armtube for extreme stiffness. European Audio Team's carbon fiber tonearm, often bundled with their turntables. Low mass. A massive, high-precision German unipivot tonearm. A classic high-end British tonearm known for its unique 'tetrahedral' bearing arrangement. A modern carbon fiber unipivot tonearm known for excellent value. Audio Note's reference tonearm, a heavily modified Rega base with high-purity silver wiring. A modern 12-inch S-shaped tonearm from Pro-Ject, offering premium features like on-the-fly VTA. A precision 9-inch gimbal-bearing arm with a carbon fiber armtube and magnetic anti-skate. En välrenommerad 10-tums unipivot-tonarm med lång historia inom hi-fi. SME's entry-level tonearm, but still a high-quality product. Lighter than the M2-12. A 12-inch version of the M2 series, designed for low-compliance cartridges and classic turntables. A high-end tonearm from Origin Live, featuring a dual-pivot bearing and hybrid armtube. Stock arm for the AT-LP140XP, similar to the 120X arm but designed for DJ cartridges. A unique British tonearm with a thread bearing and F-X cross-braced armtube. The 11-inch version of the 4Point, offering a sweet spot between 9-inch and 12-inch designs. The current iteration of the renowned RB300 tonearm, featured on the Planar 3 turntable.  Improvements include enhanced bearing design and internal wiring. The stock 8.6-inch carbon-wrapped aluminum tonearm on popular Pro-Ject models. A popular carbon fiber tonearm from Clearaudio with a magnetic anti-skate system. The 12-inch version of the classic Tri-Planar tonearm. The classic 12-inch 'banana' arm, an icon of broadcast turntables. One of Jelco's higher-end models, featuring a static-balance knife-edge bearing system. Linn's current entry-to-mid level tonearm, replacing the Akito on some models. A modified version of an SME arm, made for Oracle turntables. AVID's entry-level tonearm, based on a modified Rega design. Kuzma's 12-inch unipivot tonearm with an oil-damped bearing. A classic linear tracking tonearm using an air bearing for the carriage. VPI's 12-inch unipivot with a 3D-printed one-piece armtube. Origin Live's flagship tonearm with a hybrid carbon-fiber and alloy armtube. A 12-inch version of the SME Series IV, with a magnesium armtube and removable headshell. The magnesium version of the SAT tonearm, offering different resonance characteristics than the carbon fiber model. The 14-inch flagship of the Kuzma 4Point line, designed for maximum tracking stability. A 12-inch carbon fiber unipivot tonearm from Nottingham Analogue. A 12-inch carbon fiber tonearm, often found on Pro-Ject's high-end turntables. A 12-inch version of their high-end carbon fiber tonearm. Hand-built version of the Vertere reference arm, with tighter tolerances and advanced materials. The 12-inch version of the legendary SME Series V reference tonearm. The stock arm on the RP8 turntable, a step below the RB1000 but still a high-performance design. Italian high-end tonearm with ceramic ball bearings and a titanium armtube. The 12-inch version of the popular SA-750, a versatile and high-value tonearm. A modern tonearm developed for the TD 1600/1601, featuring a knife-edge vertical bearing. A classic British tonearm from the 80s, known for its solid build and dynamic sound.
